title: Education, Employment, and Infrastructure
description: RAND Education, Employment, and Infrastructure advances educational opportunity, economic prosperity, and civic life.
text:
There's this focus on K–3 without a lot of resources dedicated to helping the kids in secondary school that fell through the cracks. Starting early makes a lot of sense in a lot of ways, but there's also all these kids in the school system that didn't benefit from that and do need intervention as well.
Anna Shapiro
Associate Policy Researcher
Source: EdSurge
Flint's not the first community to struggle with high water bills, or water contamination problems, or not being able to trust their drinking water quality. Some of these problems have been around for a while and we've been studying them… It's been tough to raise these issues to the level of national attention that they have now.
Sara Hughes
Senior Policy Researcher
Source: Radio Health Journal
